stories.title: Secret correspondence claims suggest tensions at top of Iranian government
stories.summary: Former negotiating team member gives shock interview claiming supreme leader’s instructions were not followed Middle East crisis – live updates A former member of Iran’s negotiating team in the previous round of talks with the US in Islamabad is facing the threat of prosecution and dismissal from parliament after he went on the main state broadcaster to reveal what he claimed were confidential letters from the country’s supreme leader. The interview with Mahmoud Nabavian, the deputy chair of Ira

stories.title: From the US-Mexico border to protests in Poland: highlights of PhotoEspaña 2026
stories.summary: Spain’s leading festival of photography showcases the work of more than 300 visual artists in nearly 100 exhibitions across the country PhotoEspaña, Spain’s leading festival of photography, held its official opening in Madrid this month and by September nearly 100 exhibitions will have showcased the work of more than 300 visual artists in the capital and across the country. Loosely corralled under the theme of reimagining, the exhibitions feature work by major figures in Spanish and internationa

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: I challenge the Rothko naysayers to stand in front of his monumental art and not feel awe – Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
stories.summary: An exhibition in Florence that pairs his giant canvases with Renaissance religious art brought me to the edge of tears. It is the perfect refuge from the infinite scroll As an unbaptised agnostic raised with no religion, the closest I ever really come to a spiritual experience is when I’m standing in front of an artwork. Last week I went to Florence to do exactly that, drawn there not by Michelangelo’s David or Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, but by the works of Mark Rothko, that titan of US abstra

stories.title: Havi 3500 forint lesz az elektromos közbicikli Budapesten, leteszteltük az e-Bubit
stories.summary: Július második felétől ennyi pénzért el lehet vinni bármilyen biciklit az új Mol Bubi flottából fél órára, sőt, akár kettőt is. A BKK-bérletesek további 20 százalék engedményt kapnak.

stories.title: 14 évesen azt mondták, nincs jövője, 17 évesen egy gyárban dolgozott, most 33 percenként szállítja a német válogatottban a gólokat
stories.summary: A német szövetségi kapitány egyelőre kitart amellett, hogy szupercsereként veti be a vb-n az Elefántcsontpart elleni meccset is eldöntő Deniz Undavot.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a vagyonvédelmi hivatalról szóló javaslat legerősebb változatának támogatására kéri a Tiszát
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint a társadalmi egyeztetés során is szigorodhat a vagyonvisszaszerzési hivatalról szóló tervezet.

stories.title: Vitézy: A Dunakeszi Járműjavító csődje miatt kellett osztrák vasúti kocsikat bérelnie a MÁV-nak
stories.summary: Itthon is lenne elég klimatizált kocsi, csak a közlekedési miniszter szerint mind a járműjavítóban vannak, amit az Orbán-kormány orosz és neres köröknek privatizált.

stories.title: Orbán Anita az osztrák sajtónak: Szijjártóék sok dokumentumot ledaráltak
stories.summary: A magyar külügyminiszter a Der Standardnak adott közös interjút osztrák kollégájával, akit lenyűgöz, milyen tempóban hajtja végre a reformokat az új magyar kormány

stories.title: Csak ketten vettek részt David Hockney temetésén, de pont így akarta
stories.summary: Az ünnepelt brit festőművész végakarata szerint csak élettársa és dédunokaöccse vehetett részt a temetésén.

stories.title: Ha tartósan újra is nyílik a Hormuzi-szoros, kellemetlen meglepetések lassíthatják a hajóforgalom helyreállását
stories.summary: Irán azt állította, megint lezárta a kulcsfontosságú vízi útvonalat. Ezt az USA nem erősítette meg, de már a bizonytalanság is sok hajót visszafordulásra késztetett. Ha a svájci tárgyalásokon a politikai akadályok el is hárulnak, marad még egy megoldandó probléma: az aknák kérdése.

stories.title: Visszautasítja a BYD, hogy környezetkárosítást okozott volna Szegeden
stories.summary: Zajlik a nyomozás a magyarországi beruházáskor elkövetett környezetvédelmi szabálytalanságok gyanújával.

stories.title: A román liberálisok elnököt választottak, majd kizárással fenyegették meg az államfő által kijelölt miniszterelnököt
stories.summary: A belharc a kormányalakítás sorsát is befolyásolhatja.

stories.title: A magyar származású ukrán drónparancsnok szerint a Krím félszigetbe fog belebukni Putyin
stories.summary: Minden diktatúra hirtelen omlik össze – üzente Robert „Magyar” Brovdi.

stories.title: „Nem a politikai állításaimat vitatják, hanem a testemet” – lesújtó kutatás készült a nemi alapú politikai erőszakról
stories.summary: Szinte minden magyar képviselőnőnek van a szexizmussal összefüggő története, és átéltek már online, szexualizált vagy pszichológiai erőszakot, de ezt senki nem jelentette közülük. Egy öt európai országot vizsgáló kutatás szerint a rossz magyar eredmények nem függetleníthetőek az Orbán-rendszer működésétől.

stories.title: Budapesten fákat döntött, Debrecenben villámárvizet hozott a vihar
stories.summary: Kidőlt fák, megrongált tetőszerkezetek miatt riasztották a tűzoltókat Budapest több kerületében.

stories.title: Rendkívüli kormányülés lesz hétfőn, Magyar Péter meglepetést ígér a parlamentben
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt írta, „ismertetem a bábok eltávolítását és a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatalt + még valamit”.

stories.title: Even in this age of what Mark Carney calls global rupture, do not despair: there is still hope for international law | Nathalie Tocci
stories.summary: Developments in Ukraine and Iran show that the military superpowers are not getting it all their own way Our age of what Mark Carney called global rupture is also often described as following the “ law of the jungle ”, in which the strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must, with international law shattered and multilateral organisations hollowed out. Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Israel’s onslaught on Gaza, and the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran and Lebanon seem to confirm this

stories.title: Írország egyszer véletlenül legalizálta a drogokat
stories.summary: Egy joghézag miatt egy napra 125-féle kábítószer birtoklása és fogyasztása lett legális. A törvényhozók gyorsan reagáltak, az ilyesmire fogékony íreknek viszont volt egy lazább napjuk.

stories.title: Magyar alapítású dróngyártó cég lett a német védelmi ipar egyik legígéretesebb szereplője
stories.summary: Az alig három éve alapított, elhárítódrónokat fejlesztő és gyártó Tytan Technologies Ukrajnába is szállít a drónjaiból, Németország pedig több tízmillió eurós megbízást adott a startupnak, hogy kifejlesszék a német katonai bázisok drónvédelmét.

stories.title: Kötelező drogtesztelést vezetnek be a francia minisztériumokban
stories.summary: Fokozzák a drogellenes háborút, ebben pedig a politikusoknak is részt kell venni.

stories.title: Nem is millimétereken, szó szerint hajszálon múlt a japán gól a vb-n
stories.summary: Sorban a második vb-n van millimétereken múló japán helyzet. Négy éve gól lett, most éppen csak, hogy nem.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Middle East live: US-Iran peace talks underway as strait of Hormuz remains closed
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Trump threatens fresh attacks on Iran as peace talks take place in Switzerland There ⁠was and is ‘no restriction’ on ⁠Israeli ⁠soldiers ​to act to eliminate ⁠threats in Lebanon, and that troops would not withdraw from the security zone, ‌Israeli defence minister ‌Israel Katz said in a statement on Sunday, according to Reuters. Israeli strikes killed at ‌least 20 people in Lebanon ​on Saturday, Lebanon’s state news agency NNA reported, ⁠a day after a ​ceasefire with

stories.title: Vance in Switzerland for Iran peace talks with Hormuz in spotlight
stories.summary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance was in Switzerland on Sunday for high-stakes peace talks with Iran, saying before takeoff that Washington had “a couple days of talks” to try to launch the next phase of President Donald Trump’s fragile ceasefire agreement. Vance and Second Lady Usha Vance landed at Emmen Air Base outside Lucerne shortly before 6 a.m. local time, according to his office.  He joined U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, at the Bürgenstock resort,

stories.title: Vance says US hopes to ‘transform’ Middle East as Iran talks begin
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance laid out lofty ambitions ahead of negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end the war, suggesting that the talks could “transform the Middle East” even as fighting in Lebanon threatens to undermine the perilous U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Vance told reporters in Switzerland on Sunday that President Donald Trump’s hope for the region is that it’s “much different 10 years from now than it was 10 years ago,” while pointing to Iran as the catalyst for volatility in the Middle East

stories.title: Trump piles on Starmer as UK prime minister appears near the exit
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Keir Starmer “will resign” as British prime minister, jumping into the U.K.’s political crisis as Labour MPs brace for a possible change at the top of government as soon as Monday. In a Truth Social post on Sunday , Trump said Starmer had “failed badly” on two issues: immigration and energy, before adding: “I wish him well!” Trump singled out North Sea oil — a frequent dividing line between Trump and European center-left leaders — and wrote that Britain sho

stories.title: Four months after the horrific Iran school bombing, fears grow that Trump and Hegseth will bury the truth
stories.summary: A secretive investigation into the attack that killed at least 175 has concluded, reports suggest. Will its findings ever see the light of day? The attack on a girl’s elementary school in the Iranian town of Minab was one of the US military’s deadliest civilian bombings in decades. But nearly four months on, the Pentagon has produced no answers about why the military fired a Tomahawk cruise missile into a school on the first day of the war, killing at least 175 people, mostly children. Some crit

stories.title: Iranian negotiators suspend talks with US in protest over Trump threats
stories.summary: US president has threatened to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team unless strait of Hormuz reopened Iranian negotiators have suspended high-stakes talks with the US in Switzerland in protest at a stream of threats issued by Trump to bomb Iran, and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz is reopened. It was not clear if the Iran walkout was permanent or a symbolic show of protest. But before leaving the face-to-face talks in Burgenstock, Iran reached a draft agre

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Drones strike Russia’s Tyumen oil refinery 2,000km away, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Reports from Siberia confirm attack, while Ukrainian president says new weapon has 3,000km range; occupied Crimea under attack. What we know on day 1,579 Volodymyr Zelenskiy has confirmed that Ukrainian drones attacked an oil refinery in Russia’s Tyumen region ⁠in western Siberia, ⁠ more ​than 2,000 km (1,200 miles) from Ukraine . He said Ukrainian company Fire Point had developed new long-range drones capable of ⁠travelling more than 3,000km and they had been “successfully deployed”. In his nig

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘This changes everything’: how Brexit altered Scotland’s political landscape
stories.summary: Former party leaders reflect on the turbulence that followed the referendum in which most Scottish voters backed the losing side The decision to quit the EU bolstered support for Scottish independence, which a decade after the Brexit referendum is at near record levels, according to Scottish Labour’s former leader Kezia Dugdale. Dugdale said the Brexit vote “creates a frame around fairness” for many in Scotland because, unlike England, Scottish voters comprehensively backed remain in 2016, by 62

stories.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
stories.summary: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote Colombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Polls sh

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been gi

stories.title: Trump faces fresh bipartisan criticism on Iran deal as Vance hails peace talks
stories.summary: Objections comes as Trump threatens to renew attacks on Iran if it doesn’t rein in its proxy in Lebanon US political figures from left and right voiced fresh objections on Sunday to Donald Trump’s provisional deal with Iran – even as the US president made fresh threats while Vice-President JD Vance hailed progress during the first round of direct peace talks in Switzerland. Senator John Cornyn, a Texas Republican who recently lost his primary battle for re-election, posted a line on X from a Wal

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conserv

stories.title: ‘There’s no jobs’: struggle and regret in a Welsh town that backed Brexit
stories.summary: Ten years ago Ebbw Vale had the highest proportion of leave voters in Wales despite huge EU funding, which has not been fully replaced Where Ebbw Vale’s steelworks once stood is now a cluster of gleaming modern buildings including a hospital, a leisure centre and a college. Over the past decade, these public facilities have been joined by a public-private cybersecurity research centre and two tech firms. A new railway station opened at the site in 2015. Yet, during the Guardian’s visit to the We

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russia-controlled Crimea in deadly drone attack
stories.summary: Footage of fires at a major depot on Crimea circulated on social media. The Moscow-aligned governor of Crimea has halted public fuel sales after the attack.

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

stories.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.title: Florida college seized by DeSantis in ‘anti-woke’ push to triple in size
stories.summary: New College of Florida to acquire USF Sarasota-Manatee in deal that leading Democratic lawmaker says ‘reeks of grift’ A liberal arts college seized by Florida’s hard-right governor, Ron DeSantis, and transformed into a model for conservative higher education is to triple in size after state Republicans engineered a hostile takeover of a rival university’s campus. New College of Florida, which is controlled by DeSantis’s hand-picked board of trustees, will acquire the Sarasota-Manatee campus of t

stories.title: A Ukrainian family built a community in Cleveland. Now, they face deportation
stories.summary: After the US said seeking affordable medical care for their son would not impede their re-entry, Tamila Vashchuk and her 10-year-old were issued removal orders Tamila Vashchuk and her husband, Mykola, are minor celebrities in this corner of Ohio. The Ukrainian couple have appeared on the cover of local magazines and been invited onto morning television shows. En route to building a successful pierogi food business , they’ve met with the governor. A recent law graduate from Cleveland State Univer

stories.title: Australia is publishing books too quickly – and everyone is losing out
stories.summary: Thanks to rushed deadlines, financial pressure and overworked staff, titles are going to market before they’re ready – and then sliding from view immediately A Sydney author – I’ll call her Rebecca – vowed never to write another book after the deranging experience of publishing her first. She’s using a pseudonym because one day she might change her mind; the notoriously small Australian publishing industry does not tend to look with favour on authors who complain. When Rebecca was proofing her d

stories.title: ‘Native children belong in Native communities’: tribes decry New Mexico drug-exposed newborn rule
stories.summary: Groups say new directive fails to respect Native sovereignty amid complicated history of Indigenous child removals One morning early last July, Micha Bitsinnie arrived at work to an onslaught of messages from confused families. New Mexico’s governor Michelle Lujan Grisham had just issued a directive mandating the state’s child welfare department seek custody of all newborns who had been exposed to drugs and alcohol in utero. Some parents wondered whether medications that they were taking for add

stories.title: Coal companies to reap billions more in taxpayer diesel subsidies as Labor approves new mining
stories.summary: Albanese government under pressure to wind back fuel tax credit scheme for multinational miners as analysis shows cost to budget Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Coal companies could receive an extra $6.2bn in taxpayer refunds for the diesel they use if the Albanese government greenlights just half the mine developments up for approval. The finding, in an analysis released by activist group Lock the Gate, comes as the government faces an internal campaign before next

stories.title: Newsom declares state of emergency for LA as firefighters battle warehouse blaze
stories.summary: Crews struggle to contain stubborn fire that has raged for days and continues to spew smoke across the metro area California’s governor has declared a state of emergency for the city of Los Angeles, as firefighters struggle to contain a stubborn warehouse blaze that has raged for days and continues to spew smoke across the metro area. The state’s governor, Gavin Newsom, announced on Saturday that he was directing state agencies to provide “additional assistance and resources” to help battle the

stories.title: Starmer expected to announce departure on Monday as growing numbers of MPs back Burnham for PM – as it happened
stories.summary: PM said to be reflecting on ‘political realities’, as president Trump joins in criticism of his leadership saying he has ‘failed badly’ Keir Starmer expected to announce departure as prime minister on Monday Some commentators have said Andy Burnham is a better communicator than Keir Starmer, but have questioned how different he is on policy. The Guardian’s policy editor, Kiran Stacey, has helpfully looked at the political projects a Burnham government would likely pursue in this useful explainer

stories.title: Teenager rescues two men who fell from inflatable toy boat off Isle of Skye
stories.summary: Archie Law, 15, sailed his own vessel to save men in distress, beating lifeboat service to the scene A 15-year-old boy rescued two men who had fallen from an inflatable toy boat off the Isle of Skye by sailing his own vessel to save them, beating the lifeboat service to the scene. Archie Law beat a volunteer crew of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution (RNLI) to the rescue on Saturday evening after the UK coastguard received reports of two males in difficulty in the water off Broadford Bay ar

stories.title: Trump says beleaguered reflecting pool will ‘probably’ be drained for repairs
stories.summary: Algae blooms and peeling paint mar $14.2m renovation as president blames problems on ‘disgraceful Vandalism’ The troublesome Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool in Washington is “probably” going to be drained for repairs, Donald Trump has said, after algae blooms and peeling paint marred the controversial recent renovation efforts for America’s 250th anniversary celebrations next month. In a Truth Social post late on Saturday, Trump said that after meeting with pool contractors, they would “probabl

stories.title: Starmer expected to announce exit plan to clear way for Burnham to become PM
stories.summary: Ministers say Starmer will set out his intentions on Monday morning with an autumn departure the most likely option Keir Starmer is expected to announce a timetable for his departure on Monday morning, clearing the way for Andy Burnham to become prime minister without a formal contest by the autumn. Cabinet ministers say Starmer will set out his intentions outside No 10 Downing Street, starting a process of the UK installing its seventh prime minister in a decade. Continue reading...

stories.title: Man falls to his death during Madison Square Garden concert, police say
stories.summary: Officers find man unconscious and unresponsive with injuries indicating a fall from an ‘elevated position’ A 51-year-old man fell to his death during a concert at Madison Square Garden in New York on Saturday night, police said. Officers responding to a 911 emergency call at about 9.51pm found the man unconscious and unresponsive with injuries indicating a fall from an “elevated position”, New York police said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Taxi and Uber rider targeted in suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh, say witnesses
stories.summary: White Scottish man, 38, charged after five men were injured in spate of attacks in city on Friday night Witnesses to the alleged knife attacks on Muslims and others in Edinburgh on Friday have described seeing a taxi and an Uber bike courier being targeted in Leith. The attacks, suspected of being directed against Muslims and people of colour, began near a mosque in the west of Edinburgh, followed by incidents on Leith Walk in the east of the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Two-thirds of EU citizens back UK rejoining bloc, survey finds
stories.summary: Poll also finds three quarters of people in Britain want closer ties, with majority accepting free movement Two-thirds of EU citizens would back Britain rejoining the bloc, while most UK voters say Brexit has been bad for the issues they care about and want closer ties, including levels of integration – such as free movement – long seen as toxic, a survey has found. Ten years after the Brexit referendum, the polling by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) , a thinktank, found 66% of

stories.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
stories.summary: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United States It was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US. The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico , was filled with danger and uncertainty . Continue reading...

stories.title: Sudan: UN warns of mass atrocities in El-Obeid
stories.summary: The United Nations Security Council has called on the paramilitary RSF in Sudan to not launch an attack on the city of El-Obeid.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: Stonehenge revelers ring in summer solstice
stories.summary: Thousands celebrated through the night at Stonehenge in southwest England, ushering in the sunrise on the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere. The stone circle aligns with the sun's path at the solstices.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: AI scandal rocks the German media
stories.summary: Two leading German newspapers have deleted articles created with the use of artificial intelligence. Many fear an increasing reliance on AI will damage the credibility of German media outlets.

stories.title: Ethiopia's ruling party wins overwhelming majority in parliament
stories.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s Prosperity Party comfortably secured another parliamentary majority, defeating a fragmented opposition.

stories.title: Severe heat wave sweeps Europe, triggering alerts and disruptions
stories.summary: Authorities accross Europe have warned of temperatures exceeding 39 and 40 degrees Celsius as a 'heat dome' sets in. Germany also faces the threat of extreme thunderstorms.

stories.title: Iran announces plans to close Strait of Hormuz, alleging ‘violations of the ceasefire’
stories.summary: Iran is again planning to close the Strait of Hormuz, according to an Iranian consulate and reports from Iranian state-owned media on Saturday, throwing yet another wrench into U.S. President Donald Trump’s plans to sell his peace agreement with Tehran to a skeptical electorate . Iran’s Consulate-General in India wrote on X on Saturday that the closure will be partly “in response to the continuous violations of the ceasefire in southern Lebanon” and will come as the “first step as a response to

stories.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
stories.summary: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

stories.title: Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
stories.summary: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

stories.title: Indian cricketer, 15, hits record-breaking 50 in just 11 balls
stories.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks the record for the fastest 50-over List A half-century in history on the same day he was not included in India's one-day international squad.

stories.title: Low-key funeral held for giant of the art world David Hockney
stories.summary: The artist, who passed away at his home in London earlier this month, requested just his partner and great-nephew attend.

stories.title: US and Iran begin talks on initial peace deal in Switzerland
stories.summary: The face-to-face meeting follows ongoing fighting in Lebanon and Iran's claim to have shut the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
stories.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

stories.summary: The collapse of a Franco-German fighter jet project and a faltering joint battle tank program show how national industrial interests can outweigh common defense objectives.

stories.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
stories.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
stories.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.

stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
stories.summary: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug,

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Cynical to get power’: Michel Barnier on Boris Johnson, Brexit and the EU’s future
stories.summary: Former negotiator believes in an unstable world, it is ‘perfectly possible’ the UK can rejoin the EU with old opt-outs UK could keep special pre-Brexit terms if it rejoined EU, Michel Barnier says A couple of years ago, Michel Barnier spent a weekend with Boris Johnson’s father, Stanley. It was not some ghoulish Brexit spin-off of The Traitors, but the result of the former EU negotiator’s wife, Isabelle, being a close friend of Johnson’s French cousin, Anne du Boucheron, the owner of Château de

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Who are his international allies?
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has forged relations with Donald Trump, Emmanuel Macron, Keir Starmer and Giorgia Meloni. With some he is surprisingly close.

stories.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
stories.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

stories.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
stories.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

stories.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
stories.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

stories.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

stories.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
stories.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

stories.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
stories.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

stories.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
stories.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

stories.title: ‘I only want justice’: bereaved families seek closure one year on from Air India crash
stories.summary: Relatives of those killed on flight AI171 are still struggling to obtain answers about what happened When Sagar Patel’s mother boarded Air India flight AI171 on 12 June last year, she called her son as she always did before takeoff. The flight was due to leave Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel airport in Ahmedabad, in the western Indian state of Gujarat, and was destined for Gatwick. “We always had a little traditional thing,” said Patel, a business manager from London. “Once she got on the flight, she w

stories.title: China arrests US academic at conference for ‘espionage activities’
stories.summary: Arrest of Min Zin, who writes about Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy, comes just month after Trump visit to Beijing China has arrested a US scholar who writes about Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy on suspicion of spying. Min Zin was suspected of “engaging in espionage activities that endanger China’s national security,” China’s ministry of foreign affairs spokesperson, Lin Jian, said on Friday. Continue reading...
